2605.26413.title: Confounder Detection via Treatment Intent: A New Observational Study Design
2605.26413.paper_summary: Understanding the effects of interventions is central to scientific progress, with randomized controlled trials (RCTs) regarded as the gold standard for causal inference in many applied fields. However, RCTs are costly, time-consuming, and often constrained by ethical or practical limitations, motivating the need for causal methods able to draw conclusions from observational data. While such data is collected at ever larger scale, making its use for causal inference is often hindered by the fact that not all variables affecting treatment allocation and the outcome are observed: an issue known as unobserved confounding. In this paper, we introduce a new study design called confounder detection via treatment intent. The idea is to query a human expert who makes treatment decisions, and ask them to compare pairs of units proposed by a principled matching strategy, with the goal of eliciting unobserved variables that explain why treatment decisions differ. We provide a theoretical basis for such a procedure, ascertaining conditions under which such a study design may elicit unobserved confounders. Building on this newly established foundations, we study treatment effects of interventions in the intensive care unit (ICU). First, we show empirical evidence strongly indicating that electronic health records (EHRs) collected in ICUs are subject to unobserved confounding. By using clinical text notes as a proxy for physicians' knowledge and leveraging natural language processing, we provide a proof of concept for our methodology in a semi-synthetic environment with a known ground truth.
2605.26413.paper_summary_zh: 理解干預效果對於科學進步至關重要，隨機對照試驗（RCTs）被視為許多應用領域因果推斷的金標準。然而，RCTs 成本高昂、耗時且常常受到倫理或實際限制的約束，這促使了對能夠從觀察數據中得出結論的因果方法的需求。儘管這類數據的收集規模不斷擴大，但其用於因果推斷的過程常常受到限制，因為並非所有影響治療分配和結果的變量都被觀察到：這是一個稱為未觀察混淆的問題。在本文中，我們介紹了一種新的研究設計，稱為通過治療意圖進行混淆變量檢測。其想法是詢問一位做出治療決策的人類專家，並要求他們比較由原則匹配策略提出的單位對，目的是引出未觀察變量，解釋為什麼治療決策會有所不同。我們為這一程序提供了理論基礎，確定了在何種條件下這種研究設計可能引出未觀察混淆變量。在這一新建立的基礎上，我們研究了重症監護單位（ICU）中干預的治療效果。首先，我們顯示了強有力的實證證據，表明在ICU中收集的電子健康記錄（EHRs）受到未觀察混淆的影響。通過使用臨床文本筆記作為醫生知識的代理，並利用自然語言處理，我們在一個具有已知真實情況的半合成環境中為我們的方法論提供了概念驗證。
2605.26413.author: Drago Plecko et.al.
2605.26413.authors: Drago Plecko, Patrik Okanovic, Torsten Hoefler, Elias Bareinboim
